description: Mesoscopic (individual-vehicle) Link Transmission Model for traffic flow on general networks — the pip package mesoltm.
rules: Import the public API from the top-level package, e.g. `from mesoltm import Network, Vehicle, ShortestPathPolicy`.
Fundamental-diagram parameters (v_f, w, rho_jam) belong to Link, not Vehicle; Vehicle(vehicle_id, origin, destination, start, route, props).
matplotlib is an optional [plot] extra; `import mesoltm` stays cheap and `mesoltm.recording` is matplotlib-free.
All imports are module-top-level; never inline or wrap imports in try/except.
Build a network with Network/grid_network/corridor_network, then compile(time_step, total_time, ...) to get a Simulation; call run() or start()/step().
Reroute by rewriting a vehicle's route (via a Plugin/ReroutingPlugin or NetworkState.set_route); the engine only propagates each vehicle's own route.
Requires Python 3.11+. Runtime deps are numpy and networkx only.
Node/link flow arithmetic is a verbatim port of de Souza et al. (SIMPAT 140 (2025) 103088); do not alter it.
